title: 2% Agarose Gel Electrophoresis
author: Alicia Grealy
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdmki44w

steps.title: Weigh Agarose Powder
steps.instruction: Using an electronic balance, weigh out 2.2 g of agarose powder on to a weigh boat using a spatula. Note that gel concentration can be adjusted. The more concentrated the gel, the greater the resolution of small fragment sizes.

steps.title: Transfer Agarose
steps.instruction: Transfer the powder to a 200 ml conical flask.

steps.title: Add TAE Buffer
steps.instruction: Using the graduated measurng cylinder, measure out 110 ml of 1xTAE buffer. Add to the conical flask containing the agarose powder. Swirl the flask gently to mix.

steps.title: Prepare 1X TAE Buffer
steps.instruction: Make up 2 L of 1X TAE buffer: 50 ml of 40X TAE buffer 1950 ml of MilliQ water Mix by inversion.

steps.title: Microwave Agarose Solution (1st pass)
steps.instruction: Microwave the flask uncovered for 1 minute. A lid can loosly be placed over the flask but DO NOT tighten--allow steam to escape. Do not microwave for more than 1 minute at a time. Do not fill flasks or beakers/Schott bottles more than half full with liquid!

steps.title: Handle Hot Flask
steps.instruction: Remove the flask from the microwave using oven mitts and swirl gently. The liquid is boiling! Use oven mitts to handle flask. Do not put your face over the opening to the flask as liquid can splash out!

steps.title: Microwave Agarose Solution (2nd pass)
steps.instruction: Microwave the flask for a further minute but remove from the microwave if the agarose appears to boil excessively. Swirl to mix and examine near a light source to ensure the agarose has melted. Allow to cool for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare Gel Casting Tray
steps.instruction: Place the gel casting tray into a rubber vice that will seal the ends tightly, or tape the ends with masking tape.

steps.title: Level Casting Tray
steps.instruction: Place the assembly on a flat bench and use the spirit level to check it is level--adjust if needed.

steps.title: Insert Gel Comb
steps.instruction: Place a 20-well comb into the casting tray.

steps.title: Add SYBR Safe and Cool Gel
steps.instruction: When the flask is cool to the touch, add 5 ul of SYBR Safe and swirl gently to mix. Avoid generating bubbles. Wait until the liquid is warm (not boiling) to cast the gel, or the tray may crack!

steps.title: Pour Gel
steps.instruction: Pour the liquid gel slowly into the casting tray. Pop any bubbles that have formed using a clean pipette tip.

steps.title: Allow Gel to Set
steps.instruction: Let the gel set for 20-30 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Clean Flask
steps.instruction: Allow residual gel to set in the flask, then scrape into the bin. Fill the flask half full with water and microwave until the water boils. Pour the water down the sink and clean the flask using a bottle brush.

steps.title: Remove Gel Combs
steps.instruction: When the gel is set, remove the combs gently.

steps.title: Load Gel into Tank
steps.instruction: Place the casting try and gel in the electrophoresis tank.

steps.title: Fill Tank with Buffer
steps.instruction: Fill the electrophoresis tank with 1 X TAE buffer to the fill line indicated on the tank.

steps.title: Load DNA Ladder
steps.instruction: Pipette 3 ul of 50 bp DNA ladder into the first well of the gel. The recommended volume will depend on the concentration of the ladder. Check the manufacterer's recommendations. If the ladder is not pre-mixed with loading dye, be sure to add 1-2 ul of loading dye before loading into the gel.

steps.title: Prepare Loading Wells (Parafilm)
steps.instruction: Place some Parafilm across a 96-well PCR plate rack and press down firmly to create small wells.

steps.title: Add Loading Dye to Samples
steps.instruction: For each sample, pipette 1-2 ul of loading dye onto the Parafilm, taking care not to pierce the Parafilm.
